Notice Of Motion And Motion To Unseal Record Under California Rules Of Court, Rule 2.551(H); Declaration Of William Reed And Billie D. Wenter; Exhibits
Off Calendar Jul-17-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF TALIA KENNEDY's Motion To Unseal Record Under California Rules Of Court, Rule 2.551(H).

Plaintiff Talia Kennedy's motion to unseal records is denied.

Defendants filed a motion to seal documents in support of their motion for summary judgment on March 5, 2025. That motion was calendared for the same day as the motion for summary judgment, May 29, 2025. Plaintiff did not file an opposition. Out of an abundance of caution and for the convenience of the parties, the court even emailed all parties a copy of the tentative ruling on May 28, 2025, at 3:36 p.m. (Choi Decl., Exh. A.) Plaintiff did not contest. On May 29, 2025, the court issued a ruling granting one of Defendants' sealing motions and taking the other off calendar as duplicative. This is all reflected in the register of actions. Plaintiff did not file a motion for reconsideration. Instead, Plaintiff waited until June 20, 2025, to file a motion to unseal the records, arguing there was no opportunity to object to the sealing of the records at issue. In its order on May 29, 2025, the court made all required findings under California Rules of Court, rule 2.550(d)(1)-5, and NBC Subsidiary (KNBC-TV), Inc. v. Sup. Ct. (Locke) (1999) 20 Cal.4th 1178, 1216-1218. No circumstances have changed. The factors under rule 2.550(d) are still met. The motion lacks substantive merit.

The motion is denied as it is an improper motion for reconsideration disguised as a motion to unseal records. This is an attempt to litigate the original motion to seal. The time for that has passed. Plaintiff should have opposed the motion, contested the tentative ruling, or filed a motion for reconsideration within 10 days of the order.

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay And For Protective Order To Preclude Deposition Of Non-Party Nancy Lowenkron
Off Calendar Dec-18-2024 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing re:DEFENDANT META PLATFORMS, INC. (F/K/A FACEBOOK, INC.)'s Motion To Stay And For Protective Order To Preclude Deposition Of Non-Party Nancy Lowenkron.

Appearances:
Michael P. Lenette, Esq. of Shegerian & Associates (310)860-0770 for Plaintiff Talia Kennedy.
Sean Choi, Esq. of Ogletree Deakins Nash Smoak & Stewart (415) 442-4810 for Defendant Meta Platforms, Inc.

The matter is argued.
The court hears the argument and adopts the tentative ruling.

Defendant Meta Platforms, Inc.'s "motion to stay and for protective order to preclude deposition of non-party Nancy Lowenkron" is denied. Lowenkron is a Meta in-house lawyer; its motion makes three unavailing arguments for barring her deposition. First, Meta says Lowenkron's deposition would be "duplicative." (Memo. 2:8.) But virtually all depositions cover issues also covered by other deponents. Second, it is claimed "Lowenkron's involvement in this case was very limited." (Id.) But it is undisputed that Lowenkron was on Meta's conflicts committee and plaintiff was allegedly fired for conflicts. (Id. at 3:15-16, 4:25, 5:14-16.) Moreover, if Lowenkron's role was limited, her deposition should be short. Third, Meta says many of Lowenkron's communications were privileged (id. at 2:20-22); if so, appropriate instructions and objections may be made at her deposition. Finally, it is notable that Meta's legal authorities are largely out-of-jurisdiction and/or regard "opposing counsel," though Lowenkron is not included in the caption of defense counsel and is not claimed to be a trial team member.

Proposed order signed and filed.

Judge: Richard B Ulmer Jr; Clerk: K.Valdes; Not Reported.(302/RBU)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Defendant Meta Platforms, Inc.'S General Denial And Affirmative Defenses Pursuant To Section 128.7 And Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $5.560.00 Against Defendant For Asserting Said Defenses Without Evidentiary Support; Declaration Of William Reed; Exhibits
Off Calendar Dec-05-2024 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. SEAN CHOI, Esq. of Ogletree, Deakins, Nash, Smoak & Stewart, P.C. (tel: 415-442-4810) for Defendant.
No other appearances made in this matter.

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF TALIA KENNEDY's Motion To Strike Defendant Meta Platforms, Inc.'s General Denial And Affirmative Defenses Pursuant To Section 128.7 And Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $5.560.00 Against Defendant For Asserting Said Defenses Without Evidentiary Support.

Matter not argued/contested. The Court notes it has received an email from Plaintiff contesting the tentative ruling. The Court also notes counsel for the plaintiff is not present. Upon conclusion, the Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The motion is denied. Plaintiff failed to adhere to the requirements of CCP 128.7. While Plaintiff attached the instant motion to its meet and confer letter of September 24, 2024, it did not file the motion until October 18, 2024. The law and cases interpreting it requires that it is the filing of the motion, not its service, that triggers the safe harbor period. See Broadcast Music, Inc. v. Structured Asset Sales, LLC (2022) 75 Cal. App. 5th 596, where the motion was actually served (not just attached to a letter) two days before the motion was filed. The Court ruled that only the filing of the motion triggers the safe harbor period.
No sanctions.

Counsel Sean Choi is to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling, and email it to Judge Pro Tem Tom Cohen at tom@thomascohen.com .

Judge Pro Tem: Tom Cohen; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. =(525/JPT)
